# FM module HealthSwatch primitive: the five-category legend unit

## Context

B-split of #14593 (horizon map v2, group B): the health swatch as its own one-PR leaf — the category-color unit the health bar (#14599) and the legend both compose.

## The Fix

The per-view triplet inside the agentos FM module: (1) HealthSwatch — a `--fm-state-*`-keyed dot+label unit for the five categories (ok/idle/wedged/limited/off), label styling per the SSOT legend row; (2) unit specs — category→token mapping, unknown-category degrade (renders `off`-toned with the literal category text — never invisible), label casing; (3) NL-verifiable legend row.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Five categories render from state tokens; unknown category degrades visibly.
- [ ] Composable as both legend row and count-carrying bar unit (#14599's consumer shape — a count slot).
- [ ] Tokens only; unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verified row.

## Out of Scope

StateDot (#14593, narrowed) · FamilyRail (sibling) · the health BAR layout + animated counts (#14599).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · split-of #14593 · consumes merged tokens · CARD-CONTRACT (PR #14627) · #14592 (category-swing evidence).

Sweeps: current window; no equivalent; A2A clean. Structure-map: N/A.

Origin Session ID: a28f1415-37a4-4a5a-a3e4-b56dd6274ec
Retrieval Hint: "HealthSwatch primitive five category legend unit"

- 2026-07-04T05:24:32Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `8be3511` - "refactor(agentos): narrow to StateDot-only per review — split siblings to #14635/#14637 (#14593)

GPT's review (PR #14700) correctly flagged a scope-ledger mismatch: #14593
was narrowed to StateDot-only, and FamilyRail (#14635) + HealthSwatch (#14637)
are open sibling leaves with STRICTER ACs than the versions I bundled here
satisfied — #14635 wants unknown-family → neutral + an 'unclassified' badge
(mine fell back to human), #14637 wants unknown-category → literal text + a
count slot for the #14599 bar (mine had neither).

So this PR narrows to StateDot only (its true #14593 scope): removes the
under-built FamilyRail.mjs + HealthSwatch.mjs and their CSS/tests. FamilyRail
and HealthSwatch will be built properly under #14635/#14637 against their full
ACs. 3 StateDot unit tests green (state→token mapping, live→fm-live reduced-
motion path, unknown→off fallback)."
- 2026-07-04T07:13:24Z @tobiu referenced in commit `1bc0708` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit StateDot session-state primitive (#14593) (#14700)

* feat(agentos): fleet cockpit state primitives — StateDot, FamilyRail, HealthSwatch (#14593)

The smallest visual primitives every fleet surface composes, built as Neo
functional components under apps/agentos/view/fleet/, consuming the #14578
--fm-* token layer (zero hand-rolled colors). The anti-lock-in render rules
are enforced once, here, instead of re-derived per consumer:

- StateDot: session state (ok/idle/wedged/limited/off) maps to --fm-state-*
  via a pure, exported stateToken() SSOT; a live config adds fm-live, whose
  pulse is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion in fleet-components.css (color
  carries the signal, motion is decoration). State is never identity.
- FamilyRail: family maps to --fm-family-* bound data-driven from the current
  episode's family key — a family swap re-renders the SAME resident.
- HealthSwatch: the health-summary legend entry, reusing stateToken as the
  one source of truth for state color.

fleet-components.css carries geometry + the reduced-motion-gated keyframes;
index.html links it beside tokens.css. 7 unit tests green: state/family/label
mappings, the live->fm-live config path, unknown->off fallback, and the
family-swap-same-resident id-stability.

* refactor(agentos): narrow to StateDot-only per review — split siblings to #14635/#14637 (#14593)

GPT's review (PR #14700) correctly flagged a scope-ledger mismatch: #14593
was narrowed to StateDot-only, and FamilyRail (#14635) + HealthSwatch (#14637)
are open sibling leaves with STRICTER ACs than the versions I bundled here
satisfied — #14635 wants unknown-family → neutral + an 'unclassified' badge
(mine fell back to human), #14637 wants unknown-category → literal text + a
count slot for the #14599 bar (mine had neither).

So this PR narrows to StateDot only (its true #14593 scope): removes the
under-built FamilyRail.mjs + HealthSwatch.mjs and their CSS/tests. FamilyRail
and HealthSwatch will be built properly under #14635/#14637 against their full
ACs. 3 StateDot unit tests green (state→token mapping, live→fm-live reduced-
motion path, unknown→off fallback)."
- 2026-07-04T08:53:26Z @neo-opus-vega assigned to @neo-opus-vega
- 2026-07-04T09:08:02Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #14726
- 2026-07-04T09:27:37Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #14728
- 2026-07-04T09:57:04Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `c6d9dff` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit HealthSwatch primitive — legend + count-bar unit (#14637)

The last split from the #14593 triplet per review, built to its full ACs
(the version bundled in the narrowed #14700 under-satisfied them):

- state → --fm-state-* dot + label via the MERGED StateDot's shared stateToken
  (one SSOT on the agent-health axis — distinct from the event-kind axis).
- unknown category → off-toned dot + its LITERAL category text (never silently
  re-labelled 'off', never invisible) — a new runtime state still reads.
- composable BOTH ways: a plain legend row (no count) AND the count-carrying
  bar unit the health summary consumes (a count_ slot; a zero count still
  renders, so a bar can show '0 wedged' to confirm none).

fleet-components.css gains the swatch + count styling. 5 unit tests green:
canonical + literal-text labels, legend row, count-bar unit incl zero, the
off-toned unknown degrade, and label override."
- 2026-07-04T09:57:04Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `5f945c8` - "fix(agentos): harden state closed sets against prototype-shaped keys (#14637)

stateToken (shared from StateDot) and stateLabel (HealthSwatch) used `MAP[k] || fallback`, which returns an inherited Object.prototype member for a prototype-shaped key — so state 'toString' / 'constructor' / '__proto__' leaked a function/object into the --fm-dot binding and the swatch label instead of degrading to the neutral off token / literal text.

Both resolvers now use an `Object.hasOwn` closed-set check (the same guard FamilyRail carries). Regression coverage added at the resolver level in both specs, plus a HealthSwatch render case proving both axes (dot token via stateToken, label via stateLabel) are closed-set-safe."
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-04T09:58:20Z

**Close-target annotation** (from @neo-gpt's evidence RA on PR #14726): AC#3's **NL-verified legend row** is a documented residual, deferred to the #14599 health-bar consumer mount.

HealthSwatch is a mount-less primitive — it can't be NL-rendered standalone without duplicating the #14599 consumer's mount (the NL bridge currently reports `connected:false` / zero sessions), so the in-app render proof lands post-merge when the health bar composes it, matching the merged StateDot #14700's deferral.

The other AC clauses are **met**: five categories from state tokens (zero literal colors), visible unknown-category degrade, count slot for the #14599 consumer shape, and 9/9 unit-spec green. **Plus** a closed-set hardening beyond the original ACs — `stateLabel` + the reused `stateToken` now guard against prototype-shaped keys (`toString`/`constructor`/`__proto__`) via `Object.hasOwn`, with resolver + render regression coverage. PR #14726 `Resolves` this on the StateDot precedent, with the NL legend row carried as an explicit post-merge validation item.

The last split from the #14593 triplet per review, built to its full ACs

* fix(agentos): harden state closed sets against prototype-shaped keys (#14637)

stateToken (shared from StateDot) and stateLabel (HealthSwatch) used `MAP[k] || fallback`, which returns an inherited Object.prototype member for a prototype-shaped key — so state 'toString' / 'constructor' / '__proto__' leaked a function/object into the --fm-dot binding and the swatch label instead of degrading to the neutral off token / literal text.

Both resolvers now use an `Object.hasOwn` closed-set check (the same guard FamilyRail carries). Regression coverage added at the resolver level in both specs, plus a HealthSwatch render case proving both axes (dot token via stateToken, label via stateLabel) are closed-set-safe."
